finding: TASK-RFT-0026 dogfood showed that batch checkpoint can advance and claim the next queue head before the previous task's post-checkpoint commit lands, causing the active direction lock for TASK-RFT-0027 to block the already-validated TASK-RFT-0026 commit. The same RFT closeout also showed that shared governance files plus another active captain make multi-agent write fan-out more expensive and riskier than a single coordinator for this task family.
expectedBehavior: Batch checkpoint should preserve a commit window for the validated queue-head bundle or defer next-task claim until the previous task is committed. Team Agents should remain shadow/controlled dogfood until production cost, wall-clock, quality, roster, and pricing evidence prove a net win.
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-RFT-0026 required emergency lease EMG-TASK-RFT-0026-a24301fd74 after checkpoint advanced to TASK-RFT-0027; TASK-RFT-0028 controlled dogfood compares incremental, fully-loaded, and list-price-equivalent Team cost against paired single-Agent baseline and keeps token counts diagnostic-only. TASK-CODEX-011190 adds the pendingCommitTaskId-aware commit instruction and pre-commit staged checkpoint closure exemption so the previous task's bundle can commit after the next queue head becomes active.
followUp: Rerun Team Agents dogfood with canonical provider usage, pricing evidence, cheap worker models, role collapse, prompt caching, mixed-model routing, and stop-loss enabled before promoting Team as default.
